reviews.review_description:
One of my favourite games — I genuinely can’t stop playing it.
Admittedly, there’s currently a limited selection of courses, but DRFT is an incredibly enjoyable racing sim. Each car feels distinct with its own handling characteristics, and every track offers a unique challenge that keeps you coming back.

The game does get a bit too easy once you master it, which led me to start racing for hilariously slow winning times. The resulting chaos — spectacular crashes, near-misses, and traffic hold-ups — is comedy gold.

I know the game is still in active development and a story mode has been hinted at, so I’m really excited to see where it goes. If the developers added a proper graded tournament or career system, it would be the perfect cherry on top.

Highly recommended — especially if you love pure, skill-based racing with personality.

PS.  I am most looking forward to some kind of tournament or leader board...it would give a reason to keep winning, and match the best racers together for a greater challenge.

Still cheaters in the game. Also get boring only having 3 tracks, says there is discord for updates but that’s impossible to find.  Overall gameplay is good but small glitches like not letting me select car color when I buy it is annoying.

reviews.review_title: It can be the best racing game on Quest
reviews.review_description:
I bought many racing games for Quest, but this one is what I was looking for! Gameplay is a lot of fun. But there is one very annoying issue. I control a car holding virtual steering wheel, but the grip very often "slips" and the wheel turns into different position by itself. Looks like a tracking of turning the wheel is not stable and it's losing correct position over time. All other racing games I tried don't have this problem.
I will change my rating to 5 start as soon as it's fixed.
Or can I use a USB steering wheel with the game? It could be a solution for the issue.
Also please add an option to disable music, or change its volume.
Game is super fun. If anyone knows where I can find the soundtrack or the list of songs from the game I’d appreciate it. All the songs are great.

HUGE DISSAPPIINTMENT!!!   Overly technical and underly programed. Cars are practically the same and limited to online races that are nothing more than a dissappointing crash derby dressed up  as a drift race.  How should we drift when cars will barely slide and only then at such extremes you couldnt ride it either way.  On a positive note, its got good bones and great potential. Just needs a lot of work.

reviews.review_title: 5-Star F2P racing game, if they can fix
reviews.review_description:
I'll leave this a 5 star review for now, but realistically its a low 4.  Decent graphics, fun variety of courses, easy to control, but there are a lot of bugs.  I started a race with a car on top of me, for instance.  I trust they will fix this stuff up, the devs seem to be constantly working on the game, so I'll give it the 5-star I know it's going to earn.  
PS - needs a lot more traditional racetracks.
If ur not used to playing game like this DO NOT GET IT. I could see how this could be fun for ppl but I don’t recommend it. The girl at the start says the B WORD, AT THE START

reviews.review_title: Finally, a good racing game FOR FREE
reviews.review_description:
Lemme start off by saying i know its a beta test and might cost money later. I suggest the devs create a battle pass or premium membership for stuff like  story content or extra accessories. I will 100% buy this game or pay for a battlepass, but i think this game would reach a giant audience if kept free and once people see how fun it is they will want to pay to look cool or for a story mode. 

Now as for the actual game, when you step in it is very obvious this game is in its early stages. The UI looks amateurish and ugly and the music sounds like its AI generated. But playing the actual game is where the fruit is at. The cars have PHYSICS and you can cause players to flip over, or you can ride over your opponents if your wheels are big enough and theyre flat. The mechanics of the game feel amazing and getting into fights with other cars is pretty intense. But with some quality of life improvements this game can be legendary.

Some suggestions I and many others would love to see would be more car decorations (decals, wheels, maybe some dice for your rearview mirror haha), some avatars for the players, and a race announcer (you can probably use an AI to read player names)

Also a radio inside the cars would be awesome

reviews.review_title: Good game and driving feels allright
reviews.review_description:
One thing though, in 1st person mode, they should add some more functionality,
Mabye window mirrors that actually work or even a gear box so it feels.more realstic. This game is a go to when i want to experience a free fast pace driving game but it could be better!
Non
Nice

This game works very well in the racing parts of the game, not so much in customization or any stuff like that. Stat charts are accurate and there is a good selection of cars. One bad thing about this game however is the weird wheel mechanics where at certain times the wheel feels like its sensitivity is on 1000%, this happens some times and is easy to stabilize but it’s still sort of frustrating. Bery fun to play, surprisingly active and I actually had to try to win some of the races, if you’re worried about people talking about a subscription there isn’t any currently. I would recommend someone to play this game if they love racing games and feeling like they’re in the moment, this game is thrilling and very enjoyable.
Good

At this stage and even it's early access form. DRFT is better than GRID VR, has nicer graphics and even features some nifty tunes. Please add more cars, tracks and music.

reviews.review_title: I'd rather BUY the game than subscribe
reviews.review_description: The title of my review says my thoughts PERFECTLY. Id rather pay a few dollars to have this game than to pay a recurring fee to play, mostly due to the fact that there isn't enough content to warrant subscribing to the game to begin with (this ain't Need For Speed Online, or World of Warcraft). If you had an option of even buying the game, Id be a bit more gentle, but this ain't it...
